A short playbook on what changed in the bundler, which numbers are real, and what it still does not fix.
Press enter or click to view image in full size

Vite 8.1.4 landed on July 9. Most teams will treat it as a routine patch and move on. That would be a mistake, because the real story is still the Rolldown architecture that shipped with 8.0, and I keep seeing teams either skip the upgrade out of caution or upgrade expecting their Lighthouse scores to climb. Neither response is right.
Here are the symptoms I see in codebases that have not moved yet.
CI pipelines eating 4 to 12 minutes on production builds. Developers disable local production builds entirely because they are too slow to run ad-hoc. Subtle “works in dev, breaks in prod” bugs that take a full day to trace because esbuild and Rollup resolved modules differently. Plugin configs duplicated across esbuild and rollupOptions keys because both pipelines needed to be told the same thing twice. These are the signs that the old dual-bundler setup is costing you more than you realize.
What Actually Worked
Replace both bundlers in one step. Vite 8 ships Rolldown as its single, unified, Rust-based bundler, replacing esbuild for dev transforms and Rollup for production builds. The official benchmarks claim…
